<content>1: # Repository Guidelines
2: 
3: This is a dotfiles repository for macOS and Linux. It manages shell, editor, and system configuration via ~bootstrap.sh~ (rsync into ~$HOME~) and a few helper scripts. Keep changes minimal, reversible, and host‑agnostic.
4: 
5: ## Project Structure & Module Organization
6: - `bin/` — utility scripts (system upgrades, helpers).
7: - `bash/`, `zsh/`, `tmux/`, `emacs/`, `hypr/`, etc. — config packages copied or symlinked into `$HOME` via `bootstrap.sh`.
8: - `reveal.js/`, `powerlevel10k/`, `zsh-vi-mode/`, `emacs/*` — submodules; update via `bin/system-upgrade.sh`.
9: - `bootstrap.sh`, `brew.sh` — setup/installation scripts (legacy Stow installer was removed).
10: 
11: ## Build, Test, and Development Commands
12: - `./bootstrap.sh [-n|--dry-run]` — rsync selected files to `$HOME` (interactive by default).
13: - `bin/system-upgrade.sh` — update OS packages and submodules, then commit dotfiles.
14: - macOS packages: edit `brew.sh` and run manually if needed.
15: 
16: ## Coding Style & Naming Conventions
17: - Shell: POSIX sh/bash where practical; prefer `#!/usr/bin/env bash` for bash features.
18: - Indentation: 2 spaces; avoid tabs in new code.
19: - Use safe flags (`set -euo pipefail`) for new scripts when compatible.
20: - Paths: never hardcode user‑specific absolute paths; use `$HOME`, `$(dirname "$0")`.
21: - Filenames: lowercase with dashes, e.g., `checkmail.sh`.
22: 
23: ## Testing Guidelines
24: - No formal test suite. Validate changes by:
25:   - Running scripts with dry‑run flags where available (e.g., `bootstrap.sh -n`).
26:   - Optional: run `shellcheck bin/*.sh` locally to catch issues.
27: 
28: ## Commit & Pull Request Guidelines
29: - Commits: concise, imperative subject (≤72 chars). Examples:
30:   - "Update dotfiles and submodules"
31:   - "Fix ZSH initialization errors"
32: - PRs: describe the scope, affected packages (e.g., `zsh`, `emacs`), platform impact (macOS/Linux), and testing steps/screenshots/logs when relevant. Link related issues if any.
33: 
34: ## Security & Configuration Tips
35: - Do not commit secrets. Use `~/.private` (sourced in zsh) and `password-store/` submodule for secrets management.
36: - Submodules may require SSH access. Ensure keys are configured before running `bin/system-upgrade.sh`.
37: 
38: ## Agent-Specific Instructions
39: - Changes must be idempotent, host‑agnostic, and avoid destructive ops.
40: - Ensure new files are copied by `bootstrap.sh` (adjust excludes only if absolutely required).

(End of file - total 40 lines)
</content>

<content>1: ;;; early-init.el --- Doom's universal bootstrapper -*- lexical-binding: t -*-
2: ;;; Commentary:
3: ;;
4: ;; This file, in summary:
5: ;; - Determines where `user-emacs-directory' is by:
6: ;;   - Processing `--init-directory DIR' (backported from Emacs 29),
7: ;;   - Processing `--profile NAME' (see
8: ;;     `https://docs.doomemacs.org/-/developers' or docs/developers.org),
9: ;;   - Or assume that it's the directory this file lives in.
10: ;; - Loads Doom as efficiently as possible, with only the essential startup
11: ;;   optimizations, and prepares it for interactive or non-interactive sessions.
12: ;; - If Doom isn't present, then we assume that Doom is being used as a
13: ;;   bootloader and the user wants to load a non-Doom config, so we undo all our
14: ;;   global side-effects, load `user-emacs-directory'/early-init.el, and carry
15: ;;   on as normal (without Doom).
16: ;; - Do all this without breaking compatibility with Chemacs.
17: ;;
18: ;; early-init.el was introduced in Emacs 27.1. It is loaded before init.el,
19: ;; before Emacs initializes its UI or package.el, and before site files are
20: ;; loaded. This is great place for startup optimizing, because only here can you
21: ;; *prevent* things from loading, rather than turn them off after-the-fact.
22: ;;
23: ;; Doom uses this file as its "universal bootstrapper" for both interactive and
24: ;; non-interactive sessions. That means: no matter what environment you want
25: ;; Doom in, load this file first.
26: ;;
27: ;;; Code:
28: 
29: (let (file-name-handler-alist)
30:   ;; PERF: Garbage collection is a big contributor to startup times in both
31:   ;;   interactive and CLI sessions, so I defer it.
32:   (if noninteractive  ; in CLI sessions
33:       ;; PERF: GC deferral is less important in the CLI, but still helps script
34:       ;;   startup times. Just don't set it too high to avoid runaway memory
35:       ;;   usage in long-running elisp shell scripts.
36:       (setq gc-cons-threshold 134217728  ; 128mb
37:             ;; Backported from 29 (see emacs-mirror/emacs@73a384a98698)
38:             gc-cons-percentage 1.0)
39:     ;; PERF: Doom relies on `gcmh-mode' to reset this while the user is idle, so
40:     ;;   I effectively disable GC during startup. DON'T COPY THIS BLINDLY! If
41:     ;;   it's not reset later there will be stuttering, freezes, and crashes.
42:     (setq gc-cons-threshold most-positive-fixnum))
43: 
44:   ;; PERF: Don't use precious startup time to check mtimes on elisp bytecode.
45:   ;;   Ensuring correctness is 'doom sync's job. Although stale byte-code will
46:   ;;   heavily impact startup times, performance is unimportant when Emacs is in
47:   ;;   an error state.
48:   (setq load-prefer-newer noninteractive)
49: 
50:   ;; UX: Respect DEBUG envvar as an alternative to --debug-init, and to make
51:   ;;   startup more verbose sooner.
52:   (let ((debug (getenv-internal "DEBUG")))
53:     (when (stringp debug)
54:       (if (string-empty-p debug)
55:           (setenv "DEBUG" nil)
56:         (setq init-file-debug t
57:               debug-on-error t))))
58: 
59:   (let (;; FIX: Unset `command-line-args' in noninteractive sessions, to
60:         ;;   ensure upstream switches aren't misinterpreted.
61:         (command-line-args (unless noninteractive command-line-args))
62:         ;; I avoid using `command-switch-alist' to process --profile (and
63:         ;; --init-directory) because it is processed too late to change
64:         ;; `user-emacs-directory' in time.
65:         (profile (or (cadr (member "--profile" command-line-args))
66:                      (getenv-internal "DOOMPROFILE"))))
67:     (if (null profile)
68:         ;; REVIEW: Backported from Emacs 29. Remove when 28 support is dropped.
69:         (let ((init-dir (or (cadr (member "--init-directory" command-line-args))
70:                             (getenv-internal "EMACSDIR"))))
71:           (if (null init-dir)
72:               ;; FIX: If we've been loaded directly (via 'emacs -batch -l
73:               ;;   early-init.el') or by a doomscript (like bin/doom), and Doom
74:               ;;   is in a non-standard location (and/or Chemacs is used), then
75:               ;;   `user-emacs-directory' will be wrong.
76:               (when noninteractive
77:                 (setq user-emacs-directory
78:                       (file-name-directory (file-truename load-file-name))))
79:             ;; FIX: To prevent "invalid option" errors later.
80:             (push (cons "--init-directory" (lambda (_) (pop argv))) command-switch-alist)
81:             (setq user-emacs-directory (expand-file-name init-dir))))
82:       ;; FIX: Discard the switch to prevent "invalid option" errors later.
83:       (push (cons "--profile" (lambda (_) (pop argv))) command-switch-alist)
84:       ;; Running 'doom sync' or 'doom profile sync --all' (re)generates a light
85:       ;; profile loader in $XDG_DATA_HOME/doom/profiles.X.el (or
86:       ;; $DOOMPROFILELOADFILE), after reading `doom-profile-load-path'. This
87:       ;; loader requires `$DOOMPROFILE' be set to function.
88:       (setenv "DOOMPROFILE" profile)
89:       (or (load (let ((windows? (memq system-type '(ms-dos windows-nt cygwin))))
90:                   (expand-file-name
91:                    (format (or (getenv-internal "DOOMPROFILELOADFILE")
92:                                (file-name-concat (if windows? "doomemacs/data" "doom")
93:                                                  "profiles.%d"))
94:                            emacs-major-version)
95:                    (or (if windows? (getenv-internal "LOCALAPPDATA"))
96:                        (getenv-internal "XDG_DATA_HOME")
97:                        "~/.local/share")))
98:                 'noerror (not init-file-debug))
99:           (user-error "Profiles not initialized yet; run 'doom sync' first"))))
100: 
101:   ;; PERF: When `load'ing or `require'ing files, each permutation of
102:   ;;   `load-suffixes' and `load-file-rep-suffixes' (then `load-suffixes' +
103:   ;;   `load-file-rep-suffixes') is used to locate the file. Each permutation
104:   ;;   amounts to at least one file op, which is normally very fast, but can add
105:   ;;   up over the hundreds/thousands of files Emacs loads.
106:   ;;
107:   ;;   To reduce that burden -- and since Doom doesn't load any dynamic modules
108:   ;;   this early -- I remove `.so' from `load-suffixes' and pass the
109:   ;;   `must-suffix' arg to `load'. See the docs of `load' for details.
110:   (if (let ((load-suffixes '(".elc" ".el"))
111:             (doom (expand-file-name "lisp/doom" user-emacs-directory)))
112:         ;; I avoid `load's NOERROR argument because it suppresses other,
113:         ;; legitimate errors (like permission or IO errors), which gets
114:         ;; incorrectly interpreted as "this is not a Doom config".
115:         (if (file-exists-p (concat doom ".el"))
116:             ;; Load the heart of Doom Emacs.
117:             (load doom nil (not init-file-debug) nil 'must-suffix)
118:           ;; Failing that, assume we're loading a non-Doom config...
119:           ;; HACK: `startup--load-user-init-file' resolves $EMACSDIR from a
120:           ;;   lexical (and so, not-trivially-modifiable)
121:           ;;   `startup-init-directory', so Emacs will fail to locate the
122:           ;;   correct $EMACSDIR/init.el without help.
123:           (define-advice startup--load-user-init-file (:filter-args (args) reroute-to-profile)
124:             (list (lambda () (expand-file-name "init.el" user-emacs-directory))
125:                   nil (nth 2 args)))
126:           ;; (Re)set `user-init-file' for the `load' call further below, and do
127:           ;; so here while our `file-name-handler-alist' optimization is still
128:           ;; effective (benefits `expand-file-name'). BTW: Emacs resets
129:           ;; `user-init-file' and `early-init-file' after this file is loaded.
130:           (setq user-init-file (expand-file-name "early-init" user-emacs-directory))
131:           ;; COMPAT: I make no assumptions about the config we're going to load,
132:           ;;   so undo this file's global side-effects.
133:           (setq load-prefer-newer t)
134:           ;; PERF: But make an exception for `gc-cons-threshold', which I think
135:           ;;   all Emacs users and configs will benefit from. Still, setting it
136:           ;;   to `most-positive-fixnum' is dangerous if downstream does not
137:           ;;   reset it later to something reasonable, so I use 16mb as a best
138:           ;;   fit guess. It's better than Emacs' 80kb default.
139:           (setq gc-cons-threshold (* 16 1024 1024))
140:           nil))
141:       ;; Sets up Doom (particularly `doom-profile') for the session ahead. This
142:       ;; loads the profile's init file, if it's available. In interactive
143:       ;; session, a missing profile is an error state, in a non-interactive one,
144:       ;; it's not (and left to the consumer to deal with).
145:       (doom-initialize (not noninteractive))
146:     ;; If we're here, the user wants to load another config/profile (that may or
147:     ;; may not be a Doom config).
148:     (load user-init-file 'noerror (not init-file-debug) nil 'must-suffix)))
149: 
150: ;;; early-init.el ends here

72: (defun duplicate-line-or-region (&optional n)
73:   "Duplicate current line, or region if active.
74:     With argument N, make N copies.
75:     With negative N, comment out original line and use the absolute value."
76:   (interactive "*p")
77:   (let ((use-region (use-region-p)))
78:     (save-excursion
79:       (let ((text (if use-region
80:                       (buffer-substring (region-beginning) (region-end))
81:                     (prog1 (thing-at-point 'line)
82:                       (end-of-line)
83:                       (if (< 0 (forward-line 1)) ;Go to beginning of next line, or make a new one
84:                           (newline))))))
85:         (dotimes (i (abs (or n 1)))     ;Insert N times, or once if not specified
86:           (insert text))))
87:     (if use-region nil                  ;Only if we're working with a line (not a region)
88:       (let ((pos (- (point) (line-beginning-position)))) ;Save column
89:         (if (> 0 n)                             ;Comment out original with negative arg
90:             (comment-region (line-beginning-position) (line-end-position)))
91:         (forward-line 1)
92:         (forward-char pos)))))

298:           (out-buf (generate-new-buffer "+emacs-lisp-byte-compile-out")))

301:         (widen)
302:         (write-region nil nil tmp-file nil 'nomessage))
303:       ;; make the process

305:             (make-process
306:              :name "+emacs-reduced-flymake"
307:              :noquery t
308:              :connection-type 'pipe
